blocks.text: A **computer** is an electronic device that processes data according to a set of instructions called a **program**. It can take input, process it, store results, and produce output — at extraordinary speed and accuracy.

blocks.title: Core Idea
blocks.text: Every computer — from a pocket calculator to a supercomputer — follows the same fundamental principle: **Input → Process → Output → Storage**.
Modern computers evolved over centuries of mathematical and engineering breakthroughs. Understanding this timeline shows how far we've come.

rows: First concept of a programmable machine
Filled an entire room, used vacuum tubes
Computers became smaller and more reliable
A whole CPU on a single chip

items.label: Supercomputer
items.desc: Extremely powerful, used for weather forecasting, nuclear simulations, scientific research

items.label: Mainframe
items.desc: High-capacity systems used by banks and governments to process millions of transactions

items.label: Server
items.desc: Provides services to other computers on a network — web, database, email servers

items.label: Personal Computer (PC)
items.desc: Desktops and laptops for individual use — the most common form

items.label: Embedded System
items.desc: Computers built into devices: washing machines, cars, medical devices, smart TVs

items.label: Smartphone / Tablet
items.desc: Pocket-sized computers with touchscreens, sensors, and connectivity

rows: Receiving data from the outside world
Performing calculations and logic on data

blocks.title: Did You Know?
blocks.text: The smartphone in your pocket is millions of times more powerful than the computers that guided the Apollo moon missions in 1969.

items: **Speed** — Modern CPUs execute billions of instructions per second
**Accuracy** — Computers make zero arithmetic errors (bugs come from programmer instructions, not the machine)
**Diligence** — Unlike humans, computers never get tired or lose focus
**Versatility** — The same hardware can run games, spreadsheets, AI models, or fly a plane
**Storage** — Terabytes of data stored and retrieved in milliseconds

points: A computer processes data through Input → Process → Output → Storage
Every computer, regardless of size, follows this same model
Modern computers evolved from room-sized machines in the 1940s to chips smaller than your fingernail
Computers are fast, accurate, diligent, and versatile — but only do exactly what they're programmed to do

questions.question: Which component is responsible for processing data in a computer?
questions.explanation: The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the 'brain' of the computer responsible for executing instructions and processing data.

questions.question: What is the correct order of the four core computer functions?
questions.explanation: The standard model is: Input (receive data) → Process (CPU calculates) → Output (show results) → Storage (save for later).
